page: Amazon Web Services c7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by AMD EPYC 9R14 processors. It features 192 physical cores and 192 vCPUs with a dedicated allocation and a base clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, but features a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the c7a.metal-48xl in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance under stress-ng, as well as Passmark CPU and memory tests. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with read-only small block and large block operations ranking in the top ten percent. The server also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving throughput. For database workloads, it achieves top-tier performance in Redis SET operations and strong results in Passmark database operations. Large language model inference benchmarks show top-tier prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large models, though text generation speeds range from average to strong.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c7a.metal-48xl offers a high density of dedicated physical cores and high-speed DDR5 memory, making it highly efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The absence of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, allowing users to scale network-attached storage independently. This bare-metal instance is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads, including high-performance computing, software compilation, batch processing, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, software compilation, and Redis operations. It also excels in LLM prompt processing. This server is highly efficient for compute-heavy tasks, offering a dense allocation of dedicated resources without the overhead of virtualization. It is best suited for high-performance computing, large-scale software compilation, CPU-based machine learning inference, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for high-performance compute and CPU-based inference workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 AMD EPYC cores and 384 GB DDR5 RAM, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 memory for demanding compute-intensive and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
384 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and compilation performance
Suited for high-performance computing, CPU-based LLM inference, and web serving
